subject: rtl8821au: disable warnings.
author: Sorgelig
provenance_doc_ref: 3.6 Class E — Realtek USB WiFi (groups rtl8821au vendors/resyncs but does not list this commit)

impact.effect_if_absent: compiler warnings (-Wcast-function-type, -Wenum-conversion) during rtl8821au build — cosmetic, no functional effect

superseded_by: rtl8821au-morrownr (out-of-tree BR2 package, commit-pinned to 3a7cdb5, includes identical warning suppressions in current Makefile)

notes:
rtl8821au was vendored in the Linux-Kernel_MiSTer fork but is NOT present in vanilla 6.18.38. It has been extracted to an out-of-tree Buildroot package (rtl8821au-morrownr, sourced from morrownr's actively-maintained fork) in P3.1. The warning suppressions (-Wno-cast-function-type, -Wno-enum-conversion) added here are already present in the current morrownr upstream Makefile (confirmed by reading /mnt/source/Buildroot_MiSTer/output/build/rtl8821au-morrownr-3a7cdb591b64d99d2670e455bde67c8ab338525b/Makefile — has ccflags-y += -Wno-cast-function-type at one line and -Wno-enum-conversion at another, plus an extra -Wno-enum-int-mismatch not present in this old commit). This commit is a follow-up compiler-flag fix applied during the kernel fork's active development but is not carried as a separate patch since the driver is no longer vendored. The disposition is dropped-obsolete: the code path no longer exists in our build, having been superseded by the re-sourced out-of-tree driver which independently maintains equivalent warning suppressions. No functional regression from dropping this: the out-of-tree driver handles its own build warnings.

sonnet-verified: corrected (minor) + morrownr-equivalence claim confirmed. Branch containment confirmed (remotes/origin/MiSTer-v5.13.12 only); date/subject/diff (1 file, +1/-1, EXTRA_CFLAGS line) match `git show` exactly; provenance_doc_ref claim independently confirmed — patch-provenance.md:389's Class E 'do not vendor' table lists 9 specific commits and this SHA is not among them, so agrees_with_provenance_doc=false is correct. Fixed one label error: affected_hardware said 'RTL8811AU', but this commit's own driver tree (rtl8821au, same payload as sibling commits 3d587b6a/c70a3fc27) is the COMBINED RTL8812A+RTL8821A HAL per its own Kconfig text 'Realtek 8812A/8821A USB WiFi' (confirmed by reading Kconfig at this commit's parent revision) — there is no RTL8811A support in this tree at all, so RTL8811AU -> RTL8812AU. Note for completeness: the warning-suppressed Makefile in this v5.13.12 commit covers the pre-split combined tree; post-split, RTL8812A's coverage moved to the separate package/rtl8812au (morrownr/8812au-20210820), whose current Makefile does NOT carry an equivalent -Wno-cast-function-type/-Wno-enum-conversion pair (it ships -Wall/-Wextra commented out instead) — a different but equally adequate warning-suppression strategy for that package, not a gap, since it is out-of-tree and not held to in-tree W=1 build discipline.
